step.text: Incorporate company with minimum ₹2 crore NOF
step.name: Step 1

step.text: Prepare business plan and financial projections
step.name: Step 2

step.text: Apply to RBI through COSMOS portal
step.name: Step 3

step.text: Submit CRAR computation and compliance documents
step.name: Step 4

mainEntity.name: What is the minimum capital for NBFC?

acceptedAnswer.text: Minimum Net Owned Funds: \u20b910 crore for new NBFC registrations (as per RBI revised norms). Existing NBFCs have transitional compliance timelines.

mainEntity.name: How long does NBFC registration take?

acceptedAnswer.text: 6-12 months. RBI conducts detailed due diligence on promoters, business plan, capital adequacy, and compliance framework before granting registration.

mainEntity.name: What is NBFC and who needs registration?

acceptedAnswer.text: Non-Banking Financial Company — company engaged in lending, investment, insurance, chit fund, etc. Registration with RBI mandatory if principal business is financial activity. Net Owned Funds minimum: ₹2 crore.

mainEntity.name: What are the types of NBFC?

acceptedAnswer.text: NBFC-ICC (Investment and Credit), NBFC-MFI (Microfinance), NBFC-Factor, NBFC-P2P (Peer to Peer), NBFC-AA (Account Aggregator), and Housing Finance Company. Each has specific regulations and NOF requirements.

mainEntity.name: Can an existing company convert to NBFC?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, if it meets NOF requirement and has no disqualifying activities. Must cease prohibited activities before applying. Directors must meet RBI fit and proper criteria.

mainEntity.name: What compliance is needed after NBFC registration?

acceptedAnswer.text: Monthly NBS-ALM returns, quarterly CRAR computation, annual statutory audit, RBI Master Direction compliance, KYC/AML policies, grievance redressal mechanism, and annual fair practices code compliance.
